ops.text: getaway store.connect() (store.py:146-154) deletes and recreates cache.db whenever the stored application_id/user_version mismatches the code's — the fix-wave schema bump silently nuked the whole cache on first open under the new code, including 48 warm trip_details whose refill costs real seats.aero quota (availability/sweeps/quota_events wiped too; db mtime pinned the recreation to the first W7 connect). A version-bump wipe is fine for cheap rows but trip_details is an expensive quota-backed cache: connect() should migrate or at least preserve/re-key trip_details across schema bumps, or dump them to a side file before dropping. Recovery this time: offline re-ingest of the intact jsonl sweep artifacts + re-seeding details from the old-format expand.json dump.
